parsed.summary: This release (v0.4.0→v0.4.6) is a 6600+ line, 84-file cumulative diff with several structural risks. A core module (process.mjs) was deleted, the primary API client (glm-client.mjs) has no dedicated test file despite 155 lines of changes, and a security-sensitive Gitea branch-protection script ships without tests. The breadth alone warrants caution; the specific gaps below are blocking.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted — residual imports will crash at runtime
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was fully removed. Any surviving import (re-export, dynamic import, cached compiled artifact) will throw MODULE_NOT_FOUND at runtime. In a cumulative sweep, downstream consumers in glm-companion.mjs or other scripts may still reference it. No migration shim or re-export stub is visible in the diff.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for 'process\.mjs' or 'lib/process' references. If any exist, either add a re-export stub or update the consumer. Add a CI check that imports every exported module to catch this class of break.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs heavily modified with no dedicated test coverage
findings.body: The core API client changed by 155 lines (retry wiring, error dispatch, likely request/response handling). No glm-client.test.mjs file exists in the test suite. Client changes are the #1 source of silent failures: wrong error mapping, dropped retries, corrupted response bodies. Existing integration tests may not cover new failure paths.
findings.recommendation: Add a dedicated unit test for glm-client.mjs covering: retry on transient errors, error dispatch classification, timeout handling, and malformed response bodies. At minimum, add contract tests for every exported function.

findings.title: configure-gitea-protection.sh — security-sensitive, untested, 126 new lines
findings.body: This new shell script configures Gitea branch protection rules. A misconfiguration could leave repos unprotected or lock out all collaborators. Shell scripts are prone to quoting/escaping bugs, unhandled API errors, and partial application. No test file exists for it, and it is not referenced in any UAT scenario.
findings.recommendation: Add a dry-run mode that prints the API calls without executing them. Create a test that validates the script against a mock Gitea API. At minimum, add shellcheck linting in CI for this file.

findings.title: retry.mjs — new 203-line module, idempotency gap risk
findings.body: New retry infrastructure (retry.mjs, 203 lines) with tests (retry.test.mjs, 352 lines). Test coverage exists but retry modules historically fail on: operations with side effects retried without idempotency keys, missing jitter causing thundering herd, and retrying non-idempotent HTTP methods. The test file size suggests good coverage but cannot verify edge cases without code inspection.
findings.recommendation: Verify retry.mjs only retries idempotent operations or includes idempotency keys. Confirm jitter is applied to backoff. Add explicit test cases for: concurrent retries of the same operation, retry after partial write, and retry budget exhaustion.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs modified (78 lines) without dedicated test
findings.body: Job tracking was modified (78 lines changed) with no tracked-jobs.test.mjs. Job tracking is critical for preventing duplicate work and ensuring completion across crashes/restarts. A bug here could cause silent job loss, duplication, or stuck jobs.
findings.recommendation: Add unit tests for tracked-jobs.mjs covering: job creation, completion, crash recovery, concurrent job creation, and stale job cleanup.

findings.title: Cumulative 6600-line diff with no rollback plan documented
findings.body: This is a massive cumulative release (v0.4.0→v0.4.6) touching 84 files including state management, client code, retry logic, and job tracking. No migration guide, rollback procedure, or compatibility matrix is visible. If state.mjs or job-control.mjs schemas changed, existing user state files could be corrupted on upgrade.
findings.recommendation: Document a rollback procedure. If state.mjs or job-control.mjs schemas changed, add versioned migration with backward compatibility. Test upgrade from v0.4.0 state files to current format.

next_steps: Run: grep -r 'lib/process' scripts/ to verify no dangling imports from deleted process.mjs
Inspect retry.mjs for idempotency: confirm retried operations are safe to repeat
Add glm-client.mjs unit tests before merging — this is the highest-impact untested module
Verify state.mjs schema compatibility with v0.4.0 persisted state files
Add shellcheck to CI for configure-gitea-protection.sh and add a dry-run mode
Request a focused diff review of glm-client.mjs changes against v0.4.0 baseline
